summary_one_line: End-tag QName whose bytes exactly match the open-tag prefix and are immediately followed by a ':' separator.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A closing tag whose leading bytes replicate the stored element prefix exactly (so the prefix-compare loop drives *cmp to NUL) AND are immediately followed by a ':' byte, satisfying both clauses of `(*cmp == 0) && (*in == ':')`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winner seeds carry a ':' byte right after the matched name run: seed 4 contains `<?xml:` (3c 3f 78 6d 6c 3a) and the W byte-set at offset 0x1f includes 3a(:)x2 while 0x1d/0x21 show name-like bytes preceding it; the blocker true side fires for W (L8986 W T=22) and execution proceeds into the W-only success arm at 8987-8999.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds keep the version-string layout (`<?xml version=` with no ':' in the prefix-adjacent window) so the prefix run terminates against non-':' bytes (offset 0x1f loser is 31(1)x6); the true side never fires for L (L8986 L T=0, F=21), leaving the branch blocked.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient feedback that cmplog lacks; the two byte comparisons (`*in == *cmp` prefix-match loop and `*in == ':'`) feed Hamming/prefix-distance buckets that reward partial progress toward the matching prefix and toward the ':' literal, letting VPC ratchet a seed into the QName-with-colon shape that pure I2S/havoc in cmplog does not reliably assemble.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch requires the prefix compare to reach NUL and the next byte to be ':'.
The branch is reached after a byte-by-byte prefix comparison loop.
A value_profile_cmplog winner seed contains the byte sequence `<?xml:` placing a ':' immediately after a name run.
Winner inputs place ':' bytes in the prefix-adjacent window while loser inputs place digits there.
The winner takes the blocker true side while the loser never does.
Only the winner enters the success arm following the colon match.
The decisive pair differs only by the value_profile technique, and value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback over cmplog.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive divergence is the value_profile delta with value_profile_cmplog the winner over cmplog.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Claimed mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (the value_profile delta distinguishing value_profile_cmplog from cmplog), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply; both involved fuzzers already carry I2SRandReplace, so the divergence cannot be attributed to I2S. The value_profile delta is the only difference between winner and loser per the DECISIVE PAIRS section.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a fixed-prefix-then-':' compare in which value_profile_cmplog shows no resolution advantage over cmplog (equal crash/resolve rates), indicating the CMP_MAP gradient does not aid placing the matching prefix plus colon.

weakest_evidence_point: The seeds are stored under value_profile_cmplog provenance but seed_bisect picks by lex-min, so I infer rather than directly observe that the CMP_MAP gradient (not the shared I2S stage) is what assembled the prefix+colon shape.
